content:
First, note that the condition  
  p(5)+p(25)=1906  
for  
  p(x)=ax³+bx²+cx+d  
is equivalent to  
  (125a+25b+5c+d)+(15625a+625b+25c+d)=1906  
⇔ 15750a+650b+30c+2d=1906  
⇔ 7875a+325b+15c+d=953.  

On the other hand  
  p(15)=3375a+225b+15c+d.  

Eliminate d via  
  d=953−7875a−325b−15c  
to get  
  p(15)=3375a+225b+15c+(953−7875a−325b−15c)  
        =953−4500a−100b.  

Hence we must minimize  
  |953−4500a−100b|  
over integers a,b.  Since gcd(4500,100)=100, the quantity  
  E=953−4500a−100b  
always satisfies  
  E≡953≡53 (mod 100).  
The integers congruent to 53 mod 100 are …, −147, −47, 53, 153,…, whose smallest absolute value is 47 (attained e.g. by E=−47).  Thus the minimum of |p(15)| is  

  \boxed{47}.

message.content:
First, note that the condition  
  p(5)+p(25)=1906  
for  
  p(x)=ax³+bx²+cx+d  
is equivalent to  
  (125a+25b+5c+d)+(15625a+625b+25c+d)=1906  
⇔ 15750a+650b+30c+2d=1906  
⇔ 7875a+325b+15c+d=953.  

On the other hand  
  p(15)=3375a+225b+15c+d.  

Eliminate d via  
  d=953−7875a−325b−15c  
to get  
  p(15)=3375a+225b+15c+(953−7875a−325b−15c)  
        =953−4500a−100b.  

Hence we must minimize  
  |953−4500a−100b|  
over integers a,b.  Since gcd(4500,100)=100, the quantity  
  E=953−4500a−100b  
always satisfies  
  E≡953≡53 (mod 100).  
The integers congruent to 53 mod 100 are …, −147, −47, 53, 153,…, whose smallest absolute value is 47 (attained e.g. by E=−47).  Thus the minimum of |p(15)| is  

  \boxed{47}.
